life_history: The client, Violet, has a history of emotional involvement with friends who are facing significant stressors, particularly Yvonne, who is struggling with tenure and caring for an autistic child. Violet feels a strong sense of responsibility towards these friends, often at the expense of her own well-being. This pattern has led to feelings of helplessness and guilt, as she finds herself drained by their emotional burdens while neglecting her own needs.
core_belief_description: Helpless: I am powerless, weak, vulnerable. I feel like a victim and believe I am needy.
Unlovable: I am unlovable. I am bound to be rejected.
intermediate_beliefs: If I don't support my friends, no one will; I must always be available to help others, even at my own expense.
My friends' happiness and stability rest upon my shoulders; I have to absorb their negative feelings to help them.
intermediate_beliefs_during_depression: None of my efforts matter; I can't make anyone happy, and I am just as unhappy as they are.
coping_strategies: Using emotional support from friends as a coping strategy, despite it being draining.
Feeling guilty for wanting to set boundaries and limit time spent with friends.

cognitive_models.situation: Thinking about visiting Yvonne and dealing with her living situation and child.
cognitive_models.automatic_thoughts: I am dreading this; it's going to be draining and there's nothing I can do to fix it.
cognitive_models.behavior: Continues to visit Yvonne, feels drained and unhappy, potentially enabling Yvonne's lack of taking action.

cognitive_models.situation: Dealing with the reality of all friends being unhappy and the client's own involvement in their lives.
cognitive_models.automatic_thoughts: None of my efforts matter; I can't make anyone happy, and I am just as unhappy as they are.
cognitive_models.behavior: Ruminates on friends' unhappiness, doubts ability to be helpful, possibly neglects own needs.
